decks.notes: This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures and using burn spells to clear the way for them to deal damage to the opponent as efficiently as possible. The deck also includes card draw capabilities to maintain momentum and a balance between aggression and card advantage. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of creatures and spells while maintaining enough resources to keep up the pressure until victory is achieved.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures to apply consistent pressure on the opponent. The deck includes cards like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to ramp up mana quickly, along with powerful creatures like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition to control the board. Collected Company allows for explosive creature summons, while Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts opponent's plays. Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides additional removal and finishing power. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with efficient threats and synergy between creatures and spell cards.

The key concept of this deck is to assemble the Greasefang Parhelion combo, which involves using cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II in combination with Monument to Endurance to create a powerful board presence. The deck also includes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Sheltered by Ghosts for protection, as well as disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's game plan. The deck focuses on establishing a strong board presence and controlling the game until the combo can be assembled to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to put early pressure on the opponent with efficient aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger. By combining cheap spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage with prowess creatures, the deck aims to control the battlefield while dealing damage. Stormchaser's Talent and Cori-Steel Cutter provide additional ways to pump up creatures and push through damage. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a mix of creatures and direct damage.

This deck focuses on combining Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can control the board and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards like Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Vanishing Verse to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up the combo pieces. Monument to Endurance helps to protect your creatures and keep them on the battlefield. Overall, the strategy revolves around using synergy between cards to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory.

The primary focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a diverse range of spells and creatures to maintain a strategic advantage over the opponent. Niv-Mizzet Reborn serves as a central piece in this control archetype, allowing for card advantage and flexibility in responding to various threats. The deck includes a variety of versatile cards such as removal spells, ramp spells, and powerful creatures to ensure that the player can adapt to different game situations and ultimately dominate the match through careful planning and resource management.

This red aggro deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through efficient creatures and burn spells. The deck includes powerful creatures like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Soul-Scar Mage, alongside cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to keep the pressure on. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional reach, while Monstrous Rage and Emberheart Challenger offer ways to pump up the team. With a focus on fast, aggressive plays and direct damage, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay by utilizing efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria, Skyclave Apparition, and Collected Company to quickly build a board presence and apply pressure on the opponent. Additionally, cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Michelangelo's Technique disrupt the opponent's strategy while providing additional support for the aggro plan. The mana base is optimized for consistent early game plays, allowing the deck to maintain momentum and outpace the opponent. Overall, the Selesnya Aggro archetype aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and powerful offense.

This deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo. The strategy involves using cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to protect and strengthen these key pieces. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's board while Vanishing Verse can deal with troublesome permanents. The combination of Iron-Shield Elf and Lively Dirge provides additional protection for the combo pieces. The deck also includes a mix of lands to ensure smooth mana base and consistency in executing the combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field's ability to generate a large amount of mana quickly in order to cast powerful spells and combo off. The deck includes cards that help dig for key pieces, protect the combo, and provide additional ramp. The goal is to assemble the pieces needed to resolve a Scapeshift spell, sacrificing Lotus Field to search for lands that will either win the game on the spot or set up a winning board state. The deck also includes some versatile creatures and spells to help protect the combo and provide additional value.

This Orzhov Control deck aims to establish board control by utilizing removal spells, hand disruption, and creature removal. It also includes cards that exile opponent's graveyard cards to disrupt graveyard synergies. The companion Yorion, Sky Nomad provides additional value by flickering permanents for more card advantage. The deck focuses on protecting its life total while grinding out the opponent with incremental advantages and powerful late-game threats. Card advantage and resource management are key to the deck's success in outlasting the opponent and securing victory through careful control and manipulation of the game state.

This deck focuses on fast, aggressive gameplay by utilizing low-cost creatures and spells to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The deck aims to control the board with spells like Reckless Rage and Fiery Impulse while using creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to put pressure on the opponent. The key strategy is to maintain a fast pace of attacks and damage output to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The combination of cheap spells and creatures allows for consistent aggression and tempo advantage throughout the game.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, aiming to quickly reduce their life total to zero. It includes powerful creatures like Sunspine Lynx and Emberheart Challenger, along with efficient burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. The deck is designed to put pressure on the opponent from the start of the game and maintain that pressure until victory is achieved. The focus is on fast-paced, high-impact plays that can quickly end the game in the player's favor.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with low mana costs and utilizing the synergy between them to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that disrupt the opponent's strategy, such as Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, while also providing versatility with cards like Skyclave Apparition. The deck also includes cards like Collected Company to quickly summon multiple creatures at once, putting pressure on the opponent. Overall, the goal is to maintain a strong board presence and consistently apply pressure to the opponent to secure a victory.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, while supporting them with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage. The deck utilizes the prowess ability to grow its creatures and apply pressure early in the game. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Experimental Synthesizer provide card advantage and flexibility in combat. With a low curve and efficient mana base, this deck aims to quickly close out games through a combination of creature damage and direct burn spells.

The deck focuses on Selesnya Aggro strategy, utilizing efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to ramp into powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts opponents' spells while Collected Company helps to quickly populate the board. The deck also includes a mix of disruptive creatures and utility lands to maintain board control and pressure the opponent. The key concept is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive game plan while maintaining control of the battlefield.
